calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi for Frances Schiessl.

Mr. Bassi stated that he understands the Examiner's question with regard to IRS Code 6166 for election to pay a portion of taxes in payments, that a lien is required by the code section in order to do that election, that it is a benefit because the property belonged to the Grandfather and his Client wants to keep it, that by making the election there are less transfer costs, and that he believes the issue of other costs were addressed earlier and he defers to the Court on that. The Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Actions
Off Calendar Oct-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mike Bassi for and with (petitioner) Frances Schiessl. John Redden for Sneh Reddy.

The Court asked counsel for the terms of the Hi-Lo Trailer. Mr. Bassi stated that it is owned by the estate, that it was inventoried in the Inventory & Appraisal and that they indicated such in the response to the examiner's notes. The Court asked how they planned to pay the $575,000.00. Mr. Bassi stated that Ms. Schiessl received non probate transfers, that it was reported on taxes, that she does not want to sell the real property, that they chose a 6166 election to pay the taxes over time, that selling the property would accelerate the payment process, that 233 Vicksburg is the family home which she wants to retain, that to borrow money would be difficult and that as personal representative she can borrow money from herself. The Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Actions was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Oct-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donna Quan for (executrix) Frances Schiessl.

The Court asked Ms. Quan if she was expecting any other appearances. Ms. Quan replied no, stated that the case has settled, that it is conditioned on court approval and things that need to be done, that it will take about 40 days and requested a continuance. The Petition for order for issuance of citation for examination regarding property of estate, etc., was continued to October 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for petition to approve the settlement to be filed and to complete what needs to be done.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donna Quan for petitioner Frances Schissel

Ms. Quan advised the Court that the parties have a further mediation scheduled for June 30, 2016, and requested that the matter be continued for thirty days. The petition was continued to June 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition For Order: 1. For Issuance Of Citation For Examination Regarding Property Of Estate 2. Directing Transfer Of Property To Estate
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Shearer for Frances Schiessl. Stephen Goldstein & John Redden for Sneh Reddy.

Mr. Shearer reported that the inspection took place, that in regards to going forward on other aspects is in abeyance, that mediation was not successful but they are continuing in mediation discussions, that they are having to be creative, that it will involve Ms. Schiessl reaching an agreement with a third party, that it is in everyone's interest to continue for 30 days, that if there is no settlement they will want to go forward with what was asked for in the petition, that they would need to deal with the real property and that an examination is probably not necessary. Mr. Goldstein stated that he is in agreement with counsel. The petition for order for issuance of citation for examination regarding property of the estate, etc., was continued to June 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court asked counsel if it does not settle at mediation what will remain. Mr. Shearer replied that the issues before this court not addressed at mediation would remain but that they are trying to resolve the whole matter.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Authorizing Payment For Extraordinary Services And Reimbursement Of Costs To Litigation Counsel
Off Calendar May-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bassi for Frances Schiessl. Paul Melbostad for (plaintiff) Sneh Reddy.

Mr. Bassi reported that there was 4 months of representation (September - December), that the claims against the estate are outrageous, that the plaintiff filed a human trafficking claim, that there is a demurrer on file, that on 8/4/15 there was a wrongful eviction claim, that the case is under assault by Mr. Melbostad, that they believe that there is very little merit to the claims, that counsel filed an objection to the fees but has no standing, that they are not entitled under 1220, that they have been unable to get discovery, that counsel refuses to turn over documents, that they have had to file 3 motions which were all granted, that the claims are attacks on how we advise our client, that the opposition has no citation to any law, that there is no basis for the attacks and that the case has been mediated. Mr. Melbostad argued that this is an inaccurate portrayal of the case, that Frances had unfettered access to the office for one week, that they paid to have professional movers deliver the documents to Mr. Bassi's office, that the issue being addressed is mainly a gross unreasonableness of fees, that there is a strong human trafficking case, that Ms. Schiessl orchestrated the scheme to obtain Ms. Reddy's labor to not deplete her inheritance, that most of the damages were caused by Frances and not her father, that many actions have been taken after her appointment and that the estate may be liable for those actions, that they proposed mediation at the outset of the case but counsel refused and that there has been no bad faith refusal to respond to discovery. The Court stated that in terms of the bills it needs un-redacted time sheets. Mr. Bassi stated that they will only submit those in camera, that they do not want opposing counsel to see them and that the Court can rule on 10830 & 1220 to see if Mr. Melbostad's client has standing in the case. The Court asked if there was any potential conflict of interest. Mr. Bassi stated that there is no conflict between Mr. Arnold and Frances as they are married and that there is no dispute between themselves and that the estate has been sued for $1,000,000.00 and $5,000,000.00 and that there have been no reductions. Mr. Melbostad stated that if Ms. Reddy prevails she will be entitled to treble damages which could be more than the estate and that she does have an interest in the estate. Mr. Bassi stated that in acting as capacity of executor Ms. Schiessl has the right to use estate funds for defense and that it is likely that their punitive and treble damages will go by the wayside. Mr. Melbostad stated that they will be entitled to seek damages against Frances and that they are fine with the Court reviewing the timesheets in camera. The petition for order authorizing payment for extraordinary services and reimbursement of costs to litigation counsel was taken under submission.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order: 1. For Issuance Of Citation For Examination Regarding Property Of Estate 2. Directing Transfer Of Property To Estate
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Continued To Apr-18-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Schearer for (executrix/petitioner) Frances Schiessl. Paul Melbostad (late arrival) for Sneh Reddy.

The Court noted that Mr. Melbostad is not present and that it is trying to figure out how to get Ms. Reddy to turn over the personal property before having to go through issuing a citation. Mr. Shearer stated that they made efforts before and after filing the petition to go through the house to see what is there or missing, to sort out what might belong to Ms. Reddy, that they have been rebuffed, that the office documents were turned over but not until after filing this petition and that they do not see any other way. The Court asked if a crafted order short of a citation would be a first step. Mr. Shearer stated that an order ordering Ms. Reddy to permit Ms. Schiessl and counsel to enter the house at a reasonable time would be an acceptable first step but that a citation is a means to an end. The Court instructed Mr. Shearer to submit a proposed order to Mr. Melbostad for review, that the order should have a deadline to get access to the house and that March 31, 2016 would be the compliance date for the inspection to occur and that the petition for order for issuance of citation for examination regarding property of estate, etc., was continued to April 18, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Shearer stated that he would like to put in the order that the citation is apart, separate and in addition to the deposition being taken next week. The Court stated that it wants the narrower order as a first step. At this point Mr. Melbostad appeared and the court reviewed what had been discussed. Mr. Melbostad stated that they hired professional movers to move the contents/documents from Mr. Schiessl's office to Mr. Shearer's office, that the main issue is that they have taken the position that Ms. Reddy is a tenant, that she is allowed to use the furniture and furnishings as long as she is there and that there is a wall of Mr. Schiessl's travel collections. The Court stated that at a minimum they need to know what the personal property is and instructed Mr. Shearer to submit an order for form to Mr. Melbostad by March 1, 2016. Mr. Melbostad stated that there is another issue, that they have not yet but are ready to file an 850 petition regarding substantial amount of personal property belonging to Ms. Reddy in storage at the Church Street property and propose that Ms. Reddy be allowed access to it. The Court stated that whatever can be done to stave off the 850 petition is encouraged. Mr. Shearer stated that they have not said that Ms. Reddy is a tenant, that they have said she is allowed to live there as part of employment, that she is not a tenant she is a squatter and that they want an order stating that Frances Schiessl and her attorney can make the inspection. Mr. Melbostad stated that Ms. Schiessl's husband is not the executor, that he attempted to kick down the door when the locks were changed and entered through the side door. Mr. Shearer stated that he would ask that Mr. Melbostad stop with these allegations as he has no proof.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Authorizing Payment For Extraordinary Services And Reimbursement Of Costs To Litigation Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Continued To Feb-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order: 1. For Issuance Of Citation For Examination Regarding Property Of Estate 2. Directing Transfer Of Property To Estate
Off Calendar Feb-08-2016 Continued To Feb-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Authorizing Payment For Extraordinary Services And Reimbursement Of Costs To Litigation Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Continued To Feb-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Attorney-Client Fee Agreement For Litigation Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Shearer for (executor) Frances Schiessl.

The court expressed concern regarding this request for an order for litigation fees stating that typically the attorney provides the necessary work including litigation, requests for fees at an appropriate time and usually gets approval. Mr. Shearer stated that this is likely to be an expensive and complicated litigation, that they want to manage the expectations of the client and the court and that they want the court to monitor what is going on before coming to court with a huge bill. The Court stated that interim reports and awards can be made and that they are asking as if their firm is an outside firm. Mr. Shearer stated that they are treating it that way, that litigation costs will be kept separate and asked if it is normal in hiring second counsel to handle litigation to get a fee agreement approved in advance why can't they. The Court responded that the courts supervision for the attorney's for the estate are different than the supervision of outside counsel. Mr. Shearer stated that he does not see the distinction and would like to avoid having the court or the client shocked at the end of litigation. The Court stated that this keeps money flowing at the front end, that that is not the way the system is set up, that they are doing work that is necessary and that the court is disinclined to approve a separate contract. Mr. Shearer asked if it could be denied without prejudice to coming back at regular intervals. The Court agreed, subject to timing and standards. The petition for order approving attorney-client fee agreement for litigation counsel was denied without prejudice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
